user_query:
I need a concise, noise-reduced log analysis across two files with mixed formats. Please read both input/app_log.txt (plaintext) and input/error_log.jsonl (JSONL). Treat each line as a standalone log entry and use these rules:

- Ignore any line containing either "DEBUG" or "health_check" (case-insensitive).
- Determine severity by keywords in the raw line (case-insensitive):
  - error/fatal/critical => severity "error"
  - warn/warning => severity "warning"
  - info => severity "info"
  - otherwise => debug
- For grouping error messages, use the first 60 characters of the raw line as the message key (use the raw line text; you don’t need to parse JSON fields).
- Configure highlight interests (not for filtering) for "ERROR", "FATAL", and "timeout" to help your pattern write-up.
- Assume all lines fall within the period window.

Deliverables (write all files under output/):
1) output/summary.json — keys: period (string, exactly "15m"), total (number), errors (number), warnings (number), errorRate (string like "12.34%" with two decimals), topErrors (array of up to 5 objects sorted by count desc, each { message: string (first 60 chars of raw), count: number }).
2) output/errors.csv — header "severity,message"; include every error entry (after ignore filtering), one per line, with severity=error and message as the first 200 characters of the raw line. No extra columns.
3) output/patterns.md — 150–300 words summarizing the prominent error patterns, notable clusters (e.g., recurring timeouts, rate limit bursts, file-not-found incidents), likely causes, and suggested mitigations. Reference your highlight interests where relevant.

Use only the relative paths above, read from input/, and write to output/.
